verses.latin_vulgate: In lectulo meo, per noctes,  quæsivi quem diligit anima mea :  quæsivi illum, et non inveni.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עַל־מִשְׁכָּבִי֙ בַּלֵּיל֔וֹת בִּקַּ֕שְׁתִּי אֵ֥ת שֶֽׁאָהֲבָ֖ה נַפְשִׁ֑י בִּקַּשְׁתִּ֖יו וְלֹ֥א מְצָאתִֽיו׃
verses.king_james: By night on my bed I sought him whom my soul loveth: I sought him, but I found him not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Surgam, et circuibo civitatem :  per vicos et plateas  quæram quem diligit anima mea :  quæsivi illum, et non inveni.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָק֨וּמָה נָּ֜א וַאֲסוֹבְבָ֣ה בָעִ֗יר בַּשְּׁוָקִים֙ וּבָ֣רְחֹב֔וֹת אֲבַקְשָׁ֕ה אֵ֥ת שֶֽׁאָהֲבָ֖ה נַפְשִׁ֑י בִּקַּשְׁתִּ֖יו וְלֹ֥א מְצָאתִֽיו׃
verses.king_james: I will rise now, and go about the city in the streets, and in the broad ways I will seek him whom my soul loveth: I sought him, but I found him not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Invenerunt me vigiles qui custodiunt civitatem :  Num quem diligit anima mea vidistis ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מְצָא֙וּנִי֙ הַשֹּׁ֣מְרִ֔ים הַסֹּבְבִ֖ים בָּעִ֑יר אֵ֛ת שֶֽׁאָהֲבָ֥ה נַפְשִׁ֖י רְאִיתֶֽם׃
verses.king_james: The watchmen that go about the city found me: to whom I said, Saw ye him whom my soul loveth?

verses.latin_vulgate: Paululum cum pertransissem eos,  inveni quem diligit anima mea :  tenui eum, nec dimittam,  donec introducam illum in domum matris meæ,  et in cubiculum genetricis meæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּמְעַט֙ שֶׁעָבַ֣רְתִּי מֵהֶ֔ם עַ֣ד שֶׁמָּצָ֔אתִי אֵ֥ת שֶֽׁאָהֲבָ֖ה נַפְשִׁ֑י אֲחַזְתִּיו֙ וְלֹ֣א אַרְפֶּ֔נּוּ עַד־שֶׁ֤הֲבֵיאתִיו֙ אֶל־בֵּ֣ית אִמִּ֔י וְאֶל־חֶ֖דֶר הוֹרָתִֽי׃
verses.king_james: It was but a little that I passed from them, but I found him whom my soul loveth: I held him, and would not let him go, until I had brought him into my mother’s house, and into the chamber of her that conceived me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Adjuro vos, filiæ Jerusalem,  per capreas cervosque camporum,  ne suscitetis, neque evigilare faciatis dilectam,  donec ipsa velit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִשְׁבַּ֨עְתִּי אֶתְכֶ֜ם בְּנ֤וֹת יְרוּשָׁלַ֙͏ִם֙ בִּצְבָא֔וֹת א֖וֹ בְּאַיְל֣וֹת הַשָּׂדֶ֑ה אִם־תָּעִ֧ירוּ ׀ וְֽאִם־תְּע֥וֹרְר֛וּ אֶת־הָאַהֲבָ֖ה עַ֥ד שֶׁתֶּחְפָּֽץ׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: I charge you, O ye daughters of Jerusalem, by the roes, and by the hinds of the field, that ye stir not up, nor awake my love, till he please.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quæ est ista quæ ascendit per desertum  sicut virgula fumi ex aromatibus myrrhæ,  et thuris, et universi pulveris pigmentarii ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִ֣י זֹ֗את עֹלָה֙ מִן־הַמִּדְבָּ֔ר כְּתִֽימְר֖וֹת עָשָׁ֑ן מְקֻטֶּ֤רֶת מֹר֙ וּלְבוֹנָ֔ה מִכֹּ֖ל אַבְקַ֥ת רוֹכֵֽל׃
verses.king_james: Who is this that cometh out of the wilderness like pillars of smoke, perfumed with myrrh and frankincense, with all powders of the merchant?

verses.latin_vulgate: En lectulum Salomonis sexaginta fortes ambiunt  ex fortissimis Israël,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִנֵּ֗ה מִטָּתוֹ֙ שֶׁלִּשְׁלֹמֹ֔ה שִׁשִּׁ֥ים גִּבֹּרִ֖ים סָבִ֣יב לָ֑הּ מִגִּבֹּרֵ֖י יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.king_james: Behold his bed, which is Solomon’s; threescore valiant men are about it, of the valiant of Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: omnes tenentes gladios, et ad bella doctissimi :  uniuscujusque ensis super femur suum  propter timores nocturnos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֻּלָּם֙ אֲחֻ֣זֵי חֶ֔רֶב מְלֻמְּדֵ֖י מִלְחָמָ֑ה אִ֤ישׁ חַרְבּוֹ֙ עַל־יְרֵכ֔וֹ מִפַּ֖חַד בַּלֵּילֽוֹת׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: They all hold swords, being expert in war: every man hath his sword upon his thigh because of fear in the night.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ferculum fecit sibi rex Salomon  de lignis Libani ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַפִּרְי֗וֹן עָ֤שָׂה לוֹ֙ הַמֶּ֣לֶךְ שְׁלֹמֹ֔ה מֵעֲצֵ֖י הַלְּבָנֽוֹן׃
verses.king_james: King Solomon made himself a chariot of the wood of Lebanon.

verses.latin_vulgate: columnas ejus fecit argenteas,  reclinatorium aureum, ascensum purpureum ;  media caritate constravit,  propter filias Jerusalem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עַמּוּדָיו֙ עָ֣שָׂה כֶ֔סֶף רְפִידָת֣וֹ זָהָ֔ב מֶרְכָּב֖וֹ אַרְגָּמָ֑ן תּוֹכוֹ֙ רָצ֣וּף אַהֲבָ֔ה מִבְּנ֖וֹת יְרוּשָׁלָֽ͏ִם׃
verses.king_james: He made the pillars thereof of silver, the bottom thereof of gold, the covering of it of purple, the midst thereof being paved with love, for the daughters of Jerusalem.

verses.latin_vulgate: Egredimini et videte, filiæ Sion,  regem Salomonem in diademate quo coronavit illum mater sua  in die desponsationis illius,  et in die lætitiæ cordis ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: צְאֶ֧נָה ׀ וּֽרְאֶ֛ינָה בְּנ֥וֹת צִיּ֖וֹן בַּמֶּ֣לֶךְ שְׁלֹמֹ֑ה בָּעֲטָרָ֗ה שֶׁעִטְּרָה־לּ֤וֹ אִמּוֹ֙ בְּי֣וֹם חֲתֻנָּת֔וֹ וּבְי֖וֹם שִׂמְחַ֥ת לִבּֽוֹ׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: Go forth, O ye daughters of Zion, and behold king Solomon with the crown wherewith his mother crowned him in the day of his espousals, and in the day of the gladness of his heart.
